channel.title: Tampa Bay News Monitor
channel.description: Press releases and breaking news, Tampa Bay

item.title: [WATCHLIST: hurricane] [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Gulfport leaders encourage homeowners to close permits before next big storm
item.description: The City of Gulfport is telling people to close their permits after damage from hurricanes Helene and Milton, and if they don’t, the consequences could be costly.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Tips on how to stay safe from hazardous wildfire smoke
item.description: Wildfire smoke is affecting air quality for millions across the country. Here's what health experts say you can do to protect yourself and your family. 🌫️

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] SUV crashes into Carrollwood restaurant, narrowly missing workers and customers
item.description: An SUV crashed into a Carrollwood restaurant Tuesday afternoon after the driver tried to avoid another vehicle in a shopping plaza.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] After earlier rate hike, some Polk County homeowners face yet another increase
item.description: Just months after state regulators approved higher utility rates, the private company providing water and wastewater service to a community outside Haines City is seeking another increase.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Beat the Streets Tampa Bay uses wrestling to build confidence, character and opportunity
item.description: Beat the Streets Tampa Bay is part of a national nonprofit movement that uses wrestling as a tool to mentor young people while opening doors through education, leadership and opportunity.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Investigation into 'suspicious' death of female victim in Pok County: Sheriff Judd
item.description: Polk County Sheriff Grady Judd said investigators are looking into the "suspicious" death of a female victim near Eagle Lake.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Tampa teens accused of assaulting homeless people because they were ‘bored’ charged with attempted murder
item.description: Tampa police arrested three teenagers on attempted murder and hate crime charges following a violent spree on July 13 that left a man fighting for his life in critical condition.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Coastal erosion on North Manasota Key threatens homes and wildlife
item.description: A domino effect of coastal erosion along North Manasota Key is washing away sea turtle nests and threatening homes, leaving residents and experts asking for natural solutions over man-made structures of sea walls.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Eagle Lake death investigation: Polk County deputies identify potential suspect in woman's suspicious death
item.description: Deputies are conducting a suspicious death investigation in Eagle Lake after finding a woman dead on Wilder Way, according to the Polk County Sheriff's Office.

item.title: [BREAKING] [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] BREAKING: Patrol car involved crash delays I-75 commuters Tuesday afternoon
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] North Port Police Department trains SWAT, crisis negotiation and drone teams at Wellen Park High School
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Florida I-75 crash that killed three leads to New York man's arrest
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] $1.3 billion Powerball winner with cancer took the cash. How he spent it

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Trump vows US will strike Iran's Pickaxe Mountain 'pretty soon' over suspected nuclear activity
item.description: President Donald Trump is vowing the U.S. military will soon launch strikes on Iran's Pickaxe Mountain, a fortified underground facility the U.S. and Israel suspect could be used for nuclear activity.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] 3 teens charged with attempted murder in Tampa after attack man left with 'traumatic brain injury': TPD
item.description: Tampa police announced Tuesday that three teens are facing attempted murder charges after a homeless man suffers a "traumatic brain injury."

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] United Way of Citrus County talks about community work on DPL
item.description: The United Way of Citrus County aids in disaster recovery, financial assistance, college scholarships, and community investments, among other things.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Dangerous rip currents prompt red flag warning at Clearwater Beach
item.description: Dangerous rip currents continue to threaten swimmers at Clearwater Beach as persistent winds and tropical activity in the Gulf create hazardous surf conditions.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Surfside collapse victims' families react to federal report on what caused Champlain Towers to fall
item.description: A victim's brother and the engineer hired to independently investigate the 2021 collapse are speaking out about the federal government's latest findings.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Local nonprofit holds art fundraisers to fight sex trafficking
item.description: Redefining Refuge is a local nonprofit that was started back in 2010 and is focused on helping children who are survivors of sexual abuse and sex trafficking.

item.title: [BREAKING] [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Tampa man on trial in fatal 2023 apartment shooting that injured neighbor
item.description: A Tampa man is on trial this week after prosecutors said he fatally shot a man during a sexual encounter at an apartment before firing multiple rounds into a neighboring unit.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] New Jersey identifies software error that registered 6,600 noncitizens to vote
item.description: New Jersey has identified what it calls a "serious software error" that automatically registered about 6,600 non-U.S. citizens to vote.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] New York man arrested in I-75 crash that killed three in Charlotte County

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Trump administration opens investigation into Harvard's financial aid for international students
item.description: The Justice Department is investigating Harvard University over Chinese donor scholarships that may exclude American students.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly, alleging the company's GLP-1 ads are 'deliberately false'
item.description: Maker of the popular medications Ozempic and Wegovy filed a lawsuit against Eli Lilly, accusing it of making deceptive advertising claims about its weight-loss drug Zepbound.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] French lawmakers adopt a sweeping social media ban for children under 15
item.description: French lawmakers on Tuesday adopted a bill banning children under 15 from using social media, making France the first country in the European Union to approve a blanket ban.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Tampa Bay area voter guide: Everything you need to know ahead of the 2026 Election
item.description: The 2026 primary and general elections are here. Tampa Bay 28 has all you need to know, from polling places to how to register to vote.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] 'Godzilla' actress Kaylee Hottle dies in car accident at 18
item.description: Actress Kaylee Hottle, known for her starring role in two "Godzilla" movies, died Tuesday in a car accident in Maryland at the age of 18, according to police.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Man arrested after $96K vandalism caught on camera at St. Pete apartment building: Police
item.description: St. Petersburg police arrested one of the two men accused of causing nearly $100,000 in damage to a downtown apartment building earlier in July.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Manatee County illegal gambling shutdown: Over $23K seized, detectives say
item.description: Detectives with the Manatee County Sheriff's Office shut down an illegal gambling operation at a local storefront in Bradenton Friday night following a weeks-long investigation.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Swing into action at LECOM Park with the Bradenton Marauders
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] South Florida has one of most unaffordable metros for young homebuyers
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Can James Fishback stay on the Florida ballot? Judge will decide
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] FIU students face penalties for silent protest
[The Florida Channel] 7/21/26 Chief Financial Officer’s Press Conference
item.description: 7/21/26 Chief Financial Officer’s Press Conference first appeared on The Florida Channel On July 21st, CFO Blaise Ingoglia held a press conference from Tavares. He was joined by Lake County Sheriff Peyton Grinnell. Courtesy of WOFL. 7/21/26 Chief Financial Officer’s Press Conference first appeared on The Florida Channel

item.title: [The Florida Channel] 7/21/26 Capitol Update Extended: Agreement Signed to Expedite Everglades Restoration Project
item.description: 7/21/26 Capitol Update Extended: Agreement Signed to Expedite Everglades Restoration Project first appeared on The Florida Channel The agreement, signed by the Army Corps of Engineers and South Florida Water Management District, will speed up the completion of a project designed to restore water flow from Lake Okeechobee into Everglades National Park and Florida Bay. 7/21/26 Capitol Update Extende
